title: Human Gates, Permissions, and Reversible Autonomy · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Set human review points for a consequential operations agent. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a risk-tiered approval matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify actions by reversibility, blast radius, sensitivity, and external impact.
Place approvals immediately before consequential effects with enough evidence to decide.
Give each loop and tool only the capabilities and data required for its current goal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Risk Tiering · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops human control. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Classify actions by reversibility, blast radius, sensitivity, and external impact. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: loops human control. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Risk Tiering, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a risk-tiered approval matrix.

content.text: All tool calls have equivalent risk. This fails the Risk Tiering contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops human control. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

stimulus.title: Emergency Stop case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops human control. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A stop button is sufficient without testing cancellation behavior.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement pause, cancel, revoke, and containment paths that work during tool execution. Artifact under review: a risk-tiered approval matrix.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Emergency Stop, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Emergency Stop applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Implement pause, cancel, revoke, and containment paths that work during tool execution.

hints: Name the Emergency Stop precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A stop button is sufficient without testing cancellation behavior.
